nextest-version.description:
The minimum required (and optionally recommended) version of nextest
for this configuration.

profile.description: Test profiles, keyed by profile name.

script.description:
Deprecated location for setup scripts.

New configurations should use `[scripts.setup.<name>]` instead.

scripts.description: Setup and wrapper scripts, keyed by script name.

store.description: Configuration for the nextest store directory.

test-groups.description:
Custom test groups for mutual exclusion and resource management, keyed
by group name.

include.description: Extra paths to include in the archive.

depth.description: Maximum recursion depth: a non-negative integer, or `"infinite"`.

on-missing.description: What to do if `path` is missing: `"ignore"`, `"warn"`, or `"error"`.

relative-to.description: Base directory `path` is interpreted relative to.

ArchiveIncludeOnMissing.description: What to do when an `archive.include` path is missing.

oneOf.description: Skip the missing path (printed in verbose mode).
Print a warning and continue.
Produce an error and abort the archive operation.

ArchiveRelativeTo.description: Base directory `path` is interpreted relative to.

oneOf.description: Resolve `path` against the target directory.

BenchConfig.description:
Benchmark-specific timeout overrides used when running
`cargo nextest bench`.

Each field, if set, replaces its non-`bench` counterpart for benchmark
runs only.

global-timeout.description:
Global timeout for the entire benchmark run. Replaces `global-timeout`
when running `cargo nextest bench`.

slow-timeout.description:
Time after which benchmarks are considered slow, plus optional
termination policy. Replaces `slow-timeout` when running
`cargo nextest bench`.

ConfigIdentifier.description:
An identifier used in configuration.

The identifier goes through some basic validation:
* conversion to NFC
* ensuring that it is of the form (XID_Start)(XID_Continue | -)*

Identifiers can also be tool identifiers, which are of the form "@tool:tool-name:identifier".

CpuPriority.description: OS scheduling priority for test processes. Maps to `nice` values on Unix and to a [priority class](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/procthread/scheduling-priorities) on Windows.

oneOf.description: Do not alter the priority, instead inheriting nextest's own priority.
High priority: `nice -10` on Unix, `HIGH_PRIORITY_CLASS` on Windows.

Above normal priority: `nice -5` on Unix, `ABOVE_NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS`
on Windows.
Normal priority: `nice 0` on Unix, `NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS` on Windows.

Below normal priority: `nice +5` on Unix, `BELOW_NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS`
on Windows.
Low priority: `nice +19` on Unix, `IDLE_PRIORITY_CLASS` on Windows.

cpu-priority.description: OS scheduling priority for test processes.

default-filter.description:
The default set of tests run by `cargo nextest run`, as a filterset
expression.

fail-fast.description: Controls when to stop running tests after failures.

failure-output.description: When to display output for failed tests.

final-status-level.description: Level of status information to display in the final summary.

flaky-result.description: Whether to treat flaky tests as passing or failing.

global-timeout.description: A global timeout for the entire test run.

inherits.description: The profile to inherit settings from.

leak-timeout.description: Time to wait for child processes to exit after a test completes.

overrides.description: Per-test setting overrides, evaluated in order.

run-extra-args.description: Extra arguments to pass to test binaries.

slow-timeout.description:
Time after which tests are considered slow, plus optional termination
policy.

status-level.description: Level of status information to display during test runs.

success-output.description: When to display output for successful tests.

test-threads.description: Number of threads to run tests with.

threads-required.description: Number of threads (slots) each test reserves from the pool.

flaky-fail-status.description: How flaky-fail tests are reported in the JUnit XML report.

store-failure-output.description:
Whether to store failed output for matching tests in the JUnit XML
report.

store-success-output.description:
Whether to store successful output for matching tests in the JUnit XML
report.

DeserializedOverride.description: Deserialized form of profile overrides before compilation.

cpu-priority.description: OS scheduling priority for matching tests.

default-filter.description:
Replaces `default-filter` for matching platforms. Requires `platform`
and must not be combined with `filter`.

failure-output.description: When to display output for matching failed tests.

filter.description: Filterset expression selecting tests this override applies to.

flaky-result.description: Whether to treat matching flaky tests as passing or failing.

junit.description: JUnit XML output settings for matching tests.

platform.description: Host and/or target platforms this override applies to.

priority.description: Priority for matching tests; higher values run sooner.

run-extra-args.description: Extra arguments to pass to matching test binaries.

success-output.description: When to display output for matching successful tests.

test-group.description: Test group to put matching tests in.

threads-required.description: Number of threads each matching test reserves from the pool.

DeserializedProfileScriptConfig.description: Deserialized form of profile-specific script configuration before compilation.

filter.description: Filterset expression selecting tests these scripts apply to.

list-wrapper.description: Name of the wrapper script used during test listing.

platform.description: Host and/or target platforms these scripts apply to.

run-wrapper.description: Name of the wrapper script used during test execution.

setup.description: Names of setup scripts to run (single name or array).

FinalStatusLevel.description:
Level of status information to display in the final summary.

Status levels are incremental. This differs from `status-level` in two
ways:

* It has a "flaky" test indicator distinct from "retry" (though "retry"
  works as an alias).
* It has a different ordering: skipped tests are prioritized over passing
  ones.

oneOf.description: No output.
Only output test failures.
Output flaky tests; includes `fail`. Accepts `"retry"` as an alias.
Output slow tests; includes `flaky`.
Output skipped tests; includes `slow`.
Output leaky tests; includes `skip`.
Output passing tests; includes `leak`.
Equivalent to `"pass"` today; reserved for future expansion.

FlakyResult.description: Controls whether a flaky test is treated as a pass or a failure.

oneOf.description: The test is marked as failed.
The test is marked as passed.

JunitFlakyFailStatus.description:
Controls how flaky-fail tests are reported in JUnit XML output.

Flaky-fail tests are tests that eventually passed on retry but are configured
with `flaky-result = "fail"`. This setting controls whether they appear as
failures or successes in the JUnit report.

oneOf.description:
Report flaky-fail tests as failures with `<failure>` and
`<flakyFailure>` elements.
Report flaky-fail tests as successes, identical to flaky-pass tests.

flaky-fail-status.description: How flaky-fail tests are reported in the JUnit XML report.

path.description:
Path to write the JUnit XML report to. If unset, JUnit reporting is
disabled.

report-name.description: Name for the JUnit XML report.

store-failure-output.description: Whether to store failed test output in the JUnit XML report.

store-success-output.description: Whether to store successful test output in the JUnit XML report.

LeakTimeoutResult.description: Whether to mark a test as passed or failed when the leak timeout elapses.

oneOf.description: Mark the test as failed.
Mark the test as passed.

ScriptCommandRelativeTo.description:
Base directory a relative script program is resolved against.

If specified, the program is joined with the provided path.

oneOf.description: Use the program path as-is, without joining.
Resolve the program against the workspace root.
Resolve the program against the target directory.

ScriptConfig.description: Setup and wrapper scripts defined in nextest configuration.

setup.description: Setup scripts, keyed by script name.

wrapper.description: Wrapper scripts, keyed by script name.

ScriptId.description: The name of a configuration script.

SetupScriptConfig.description:
Deserialized form of setup script configuration before compilation.

This is defined as a top-level element.

capture-stderr.description: Whether to capture stderr from this setup script.

capture-stdout.description: Whether to capture stdout from this setup script.

command.description: The command to run for this setup script.

junit.description: JUnit XML output settings for this setup script.

leak-timeout.description: Leak-timeout configuration for this setup script.

slow-timeout.description: Slow-timeout configuration for this setup script.

SetupScriptJunitConfig.description: JUnit XML output settings for a setup script.

store-failure-output.description:
Whether to store this setup script's output on failure in the JUnit XML
report. Defaults to true.

store-success-output.description:
Whether to store this setup script's output on success in the JUnit XML
report. Defaults to true.

SlowTimeoutResult.description:
The result of controlling slow timeout behavior.

In most situations a timed out test should be marked failing. However, there are certain
classes of tests which are expected to run indefinitely long, like fuzzing, which explores a
huge state space. For these tests it's nice to be able to treat a timeout as a success since
they generally check for invariants and other properties of the code under test during their
execution. A timeout in this context doesn't mean that there are no failing inputs, it just
means that they weren't found up until that moment, which is still valuable information.

oneOf.description: The test is marked as failed.
The test is marked as passed.

StatusLevel.description:
Level of status information to display during test runs.

Status levels are incremental: each level causes all the statuses listed
above it to be output. For example, `slow` implies `retry` and `fail`.

oneOf.description: No output.
Only output test failures.
Output test retries; includes `fail`.
Output slow tests; includes `retry`.
Output leaky tests; includes `slow`.
Output passing tests; includes `leak`.
Output skipped tests; includes `pass`.
Equivalent to `"skip"` today; reserved for future expansion.

dir.description: Directory where nextest stores its data.

TerminateMode.description: Mode for terminating running tests when max-fail is exceeded.

oneOf.description: Wait for running tests to complete naturally.
Send termination signals to running tests immediately.

TestGroupConfig.description: Configuration for a single test group.

max-threads.description: Maximum number of threads this test group may use concurrently.

TestOutputDisplay.description: When to display test output in the reporter.

oneOf.description: Shows captured output as soon as the test completes.

Shows captured output when the test completes and again at the end of
the run.
Shows captured output only at the end of the run.
Never shows captured output.

TestPriority.description: A test priority between -100 and 100, inclusive. Higher values run sooner.

WrapperScriptConfig.description:
Deserialized form of wrapper script configuration before compilation.

This is defined as a top-level element.

command.description: The command to run as the wrapper.

target-runner.description: How this wrapper composes with a configured target runner.

WrapperScriptTargetRunner.description: How a wrapper script composes with a configured target runner.

oneOf.description: The target runner is ignored.

When a target runner is configured, it replaces the wrapper; otherwise
the wrapper runs as usual.

The target runner runs within the wrapper script. The command line used
is `<wrapper> <target-runner> <test-binary> <args>`.

The target runner runs around the wrapper script. The command line used
is `<target-runner> <wrapper> <test-binary> <args>`.
